随着备受争议的人工智能公司在英国扩张，纽约市医院放弃了 Palantir

New York City hospitals drop Palantir as controversial AI firm expands in UK

www.theguardian.com 作者 chrisjj 分数 283 评论 136 2026/03/27 04:35
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 由于英国对这家数据分析和人工智能公司的政府合同存在争议，纽约市公立医院系统宣布将不再与 Palantir 续签合同。美国最大的市政公共医疗系统总裁米切尔·卡茨 (Mitchell Katz) 博士上周在纽约市议会作证时表示，与 Palantir 的协议将于 10 月到期。他在听证会上表示，这份专注于为保险索赔追回资金的合同始终是短期的，并且存在“绝对防火墙”阻止 Palantir 与美国移民和海关执法部门共享信息。他表示，该机构“没有发生任何事件”。由美国朋友服务委员会与《卫报》分享并由 Intercept 最先报道的合同和相关付款文件显示，自 2023 年 11 月以来，NYC Health + Hospitals 已向 Palantir 支付了近 400 万美元。合同指出，Palantir 将能够审查有关患者健康状况的记录，并帮助医院通过医疗补助等计划索取更多公共福利资金。它还包括一行内容，指出在市政府机构的许可下，Palantir 可以对患者受保护的健康信息进行“去识别化”，并将其用于“研究以外的目的”。 NYC Health + Hospitals 在给《卫报》的一封电子邮件中表示，它将过渡到完全内部制作的系统，合同到期后不会与 Palantir 共享数据或使用该公司的应用程序。该机构在一份电子邮件声明中表示：“NYC Health + Hospitals 对 Palantir 技术的使用严格限于收入周期优化，帮助公共医疗保健系统缩小提供的服务和收取的费用之间的差距，保护关键收入，并减少可避免的拒绝。” Palantir 发言人在一份声明中表示：“Palantir 作为一家软件公司，不拥有或拥有对客户数据的任何权利，每个客户环境都通过强大的安全控制措施单独保护，防止未经授权的访问或滥用，这些安全控制措施可以由机构全面管理和审计。 Palantir 在英国的业务不断增长随着纽约市的医院系统准备与 Palantir 分道扬镳，该公司在与英国国家医疗服务体系 (NHS) 签订的价值 3.3 亿英镑的协议中也面临着类似的隐私问题审查。英国卫生官员担心，围绕 Palantir 的争议可能会阻止该公司数据系统在全国范围内的推广，尽管 Keir Starmer 正在努力加快部署速度。截至去年夏天，由于社区和医生的担忧，该国甚至没有一半的卫生当局开始使用 Palantir 的技术。健康正义慈善机构 Medact 在 3 月 12 日的简报中表示，Palantir 的软件可能会导致“数据驱动的国家滥用权力”，包括美国式的 ICE 突击搜查。 Palantir 否认可以以这种方式使用这些数据，并指出这是非法的，并且违反了合同。 Palantir 还与英国政府国防部签订了合同，尽管遭到活动人士和一些立法者的强烈反对，但它正在扩大其在该国的影响力。 《卫报》上周透露，Palantir 正试图获取敏感的国家金融监管数据。金融行为监管局是从银行到对冲基金等数千家金融机构的监管机构，授予 Palantir 一份调查内部情报数据的合同，以帮助根除金融犯罪。这引发了一些议员的强烈抗议，他们敦促政府停止这项协议。自由民主党周一呼吁政府对该合同进行调查。 Starmer 驳斥了有关英国对包括 Palantir 在内的美国科技公司“危险地过度依赖”的说法，但指出他更希望拥有更多的国内能力。Medact 在英国引发了对 Palantir 访问去识别化患者数据的能力的隐私担忧。 （去识别化数据是指被剥夺了可以表明个人身份的特征的数据，例如姓名和社会安全号码）。在 3 月 12 日向卫生官员举行的简报中，Medact 认为 NHS 的数据隐私保护不足；英国 NHS 表示，数据在通过其国家软件系统 NHS 联合数据平台 (FDP) 时会被去识别化。但 Medact 担心这些数据很容易被重新识别。 NHS 发言人在给《卫报》的电子邮件声明中表示，FDP 的供应商“是根据公共合同规定指定的，并且只能在 NHS 的指示下运营，所有数据访问仍处于 NHS 的控制之下，并有严格的合同义务保护机密性”。 数据隐私问题 接受《卫报》采访的数据隐私专家表示，Palantir 出于其他目的访问纽约人的去识别化数据存在风险凯斯西储大学法学教授莎罗娜·霍夫曼 (Sharona Hoffman) 表示：“去识别化已不再是过去的保证，人工智能功能重新识别信息变得更加容易。”加州大学欧文分校法学教授阿里·埃兹拉·瓦尔德曼 (Ari Ezra Waldman) 研究了政府和科技公司如何使用个人数据，他表示我们应该感到担忧。 “每当像 Palantir 这样的公司或敌对政府收集有关弱势群体的信息时”。他特别担心合同中关于将信息用于“研究以外的目的”的条款。他说，这告诉他，政府在谈判合同时没有足够的权力来阻止 Palantir，或者不关心或不知道风险。活动人士声称取得了胜利，尽管医院系统声称这种合作伙伴关系对患者没有真正的风险，但居住在纽约市及其他地区的活动人士认为这是一场胜利。护士、亲巴勒斯坦活动人士以及社会和气候正义团体向市政府施加压力，作为名为“清除”的全国性运动的一部分Palantir 阻止该公司与政府机构、大学和企业签订合同。美国朋友服务委员会组织者肯尼·莫里斯 (Kenny Morris) 表示：“我们认为，针对美国 ICE 移民以及在伊朗选择轰炸地点的人工智能系统不应该与医院中使用的人工智能系统相同。”该组织通过公共记录请求获得了 Palantir 与 NYC Health + Hospitals 的合同，并与 Intercept 和卫报分享了该文件。全国护士工会和抵制、撤资和制裁 (BDS) 运动也参与了这场运动。英国发起“我们的 NHS 中没有 Palantir”运动的团体希望纽约市公立医院系统让 Palantir 合同到期的决定也能助长他们自己的斗争。 Medact 和英国国际特赦组织在电子邮件声明中告诉卫报，他们呼吁 NHS 效仿纽约市的做法，终止与 Palantir 的 3.3 亿英镑合同。“正如纽约的活动人士所表明的那样，工人和社区可以让我们的医疗机构承担责任，并推动他们做出正确的选择。我们也会在这里做同样的事情，并迫使英国 NHS 取消这份合同。”Medact 企业活动负责人 Rhiannon Mihranian Osborne 博士与 Purge 保持联系。帕兰提尔。

Original Summary

  1. New York City’s public hospital system announced that it would not be renewing its contract with Palantir as controversy mounts in the UK over the data analytics and AI firm’s government contract.The president of the US’ largest municipal public healthcare system, Dr Mitchell Katz, testified last week before the New York city council that the agreement with Palantir would expire in October.He said at the hearing that the contract, which focused on recovering money for insurance claims, was always meant to be short-term, and that there was an “absolute firewall” preventing Palantir from sharing information with US Immigration and Customs Enforcement. He said that the agency has “not had any incidents”.The contract and related payment documents shared with the Guardian by the American Friends Service Committee and first reported by the Intercept, show that NYC Health + Hospitals has paid Palantir nearly $4m since November 2023. The contract noted that Palantir would be able to review notes about patient’s health and help the hospital claim more money in public benefits through programs such as Medicaid. It also includes a line stating that with permission from the city agency, Palantir can “de-identify” patients’ protected health information and use it for “purposes other than research”.NYC Health + Hospitals said in an email to the Guardian that it will be transitioning to systems that were made entirely in-house, and there will be no data shared with Palantir or use of the company’s applications after the contract expires. “NYC Health + Hospitals’ use of Palantir technology is strictly limited to revenue cycle optimization, helping the public healthcare system close gaps between services delivered and charges captured, protect critical revenue, and reduce avoidable denials,” the agency said in an emailed statement.A Palantir spokesperson said in a statement: “Palantir, as a software company, does not own or have any rights to customer data – and each customer environment is individually protected against unauthorized access or misuse via robust security controls which can be fully administered and audited by the customer.”Palantir presence grows in the UKAs New York City’s hospital system prepares to part ways with Palantir, the company is facing similar scrutiny over privacy issues in its £330m agreement with the UK’s National Health Service (NHS). Health officials in the UK are concerned that the controversy surrounding Palantir may stop the nationwide rollout of the company’s data system, even though Keir Starmer is trying to speed up deployment. As of last summer, not even half of the country’s health authorities had started using Palantir’s technology amid concerns from the community and doctors. A 12 March briefing by Medact, a health justice charity, said Palantir’s software could enable “data-driven state abuses of power”, including US-style ICE raids. Palantir has denied that the data could be used in this way, noting that it would be illegal and a breach of contract.Palantir, which also contracts with the British government’s Ministry of Defence, is expanding its influence in the country – despite backlash from activists and some lawmakers. The Guardian revealed last week that Palantir is trying to gain access to sensitive national financial regulation data.The Financial Conduct Authority, a watchdog for thousands of financial bodies from banks to hedge funds, awarded Palantir a contract to investigate internal intelligence data to help root out financial crime. That has sparked outcry from some MPs, who have urged the government to halt this agreement. Liberal Democrats called on Monday for a government investigation into the contract. Starmer has dismissed suggestions that the UK has become “dangerously over-reliant” on American tech companies, including Palantir, but noted he preferred to have more domestic capability.Medact has raised privacy concerns in the UK about Palantir’s ability to access de-identified patient data. (De-identified data refers to data that has been stripped of characteristics that could indicate who an individual is, such as names and social security numbers). In a 12 March briefing for health officials, Medact argued that the NHS’s data privacy protections are insufficient; NHS England has said that data is de-identified as it moves through its national software system, the NHS federated data platform (FDP). But Medact cited concerns that this data can be easily re-identified.An NHS spokesperson said in an emailed statement to the Guardian that the supplier of the FDP “was appointed in line with public contract regulations and must only operate under the instruction of the NHS, with all access to data remaining under NHS control and strict contractual obligations protecting confidentiality”.Data privacy concernsData privacy experts interviewed by the Guardian said that there are risks in Palantir accessing New Yorkers’ de-identified data for purposes other than research, especially given the company’s vast access to government records, willingness to cooperate with the federal government and ability to connect and analyze large datasets.“De-identification is not the guarantee it used to be, and it’s getting easier with AI capabilities to re-identify information,” said Sharona Hoffman, a law professor at Case Western Reserve University.Ari Ezra Waldman, a law professor at UC Irvine who has researched how governments and tech companies use data about individuals, says that we should be concerned “whenever a company like Palantir or a hostile government collects information on vulnerable populations”. He’s particularly concerned about the contract’s provision to use the information for “purposes other than research”. That tells him the government didn’t have enough power to push back on Palantir when negotiating the contract, or didn’t care or know the risk, he says.Activists claim a victoryDespite the hospital system’s claims that the partnership had no real risks for patients, activists living in New York City, and beyond, are counting this as a win.Nurses, pro-Palestinian activists and social and climate justice groups applied pressure on the city government as part of a nationwide campaign known as Purge Palantir to stop the company from contracting with government agencies, universities and corporations.“We don’t think that the same AI systems that are targeting immigrants here in the United States for ICE, as well as choosing places to bomb in Iran, should be the same AI systems used in hospitals,” said Kenny Morris, an organizer with the American Friends Service Committee. The group obtained the NYC Health + Hospitals contract with Palantir through a public records request, and shared the document with the Intercept and the Guardian. The national nurses union and the Boycott, Divestment, and Sanctions (BDS) movement were also involved in the campaign.Groups with the “No Palantir in our NHS” campaign in the UK are hoping New York City’s public hospital system’s decision to let the Palantir contract expire fuels their own fight, too. Medact and Amnesty International UK told the Guardian in emailed statements that they are calling on the NHS to follow New York City’s example and terminate its £330m contract with Palantir.“As campaigners in New York have shown, workers and communities can hold our health institutions accountable and push them to make the right choice. We will do the same here, and force NHS England to cancel this contract,” Dr Rhiannon Mihranian Osborne, corporate campaigns lead at Medact, which is in touch with Purge Palantir.

luma 2026/03/27 08:31
阿门。

如今，人们似乎对私有数据中心的概念感到困惑。 Palantir 的客户往往是对自己的数据非常偏执的组织，如果没有信心这些问题得到解决，他们不会将数据交给一些笨蛋。 军队和政府通常不会处理情报数据之类的事情，所以我认为 Palantir 能够向世界上最偏执的组织提供令人信服的理由，证明他们的数据不会被发送到任何地方（而且无论如何它都可能是气隙的），这是合理的。

仅仅因为您接触的所有内容都在云中，并不意味着其他组织不再构建自己的数据中心，然后购买在内部运行的软件。

Amen.

People seem to struggle with the concept of private datacenters these days. Palantir customers tend to be the sorts of orgs that are pretty paranoid about their data, and they wouldn't be handing it over to some schmucks without being confident that those concerns were addressed. Militaries and governments generally aren't fuckin around with things like intelligence data, so I think it's reasonable that Palantir is able to make a convincing case to the world's most paranoid orgs that their data isn't being sent anywhere (and it'd likely be air gapped anyway).

Just because everything you touch is in the cloud doesn't mean other orgs aren't still building their own datacenters and then buying software to run inside.

根据文章：

> 它还包括一行内容，指出经过市政府机构的许可，Palantir 可以“去识别化”患者受保护的健康信息，并将其用于“研究以外的目的”。

根据 HIPPA，“研究”有一个非常具体的定义，这使得“研究以外的目的”变得相当广泛。是的，这是“经过许可”的，但这确实取决于市政府机构是否充分了解 Palantir 在离开所覆盖实体且未经进一步明确许可的情况下可以使用去识别化数据执行哪些辅助操作。

According to the article:

> It also includes a line stating that with permission from the city agency, Palantir can “de-identify” patients’ protected health information and use it for “purposes other than research”.

Under HIPPA, "research" has a very specific definition which renders "purposes other than research" quite broad. Yes, it's "with permission" but it does depend on the city agency fully understanding what ancillary things Palantir can do with de-identified data once it has left the covered entity and without further explicit permission.

jazzyjackson 2026/03/27 08:09
我真的从未听说过本地 365 部署，我认为任何机密性都是通过合同承诺来处理的，违反承诺将产生法律后果。以 Azure GovCloud 为例，在一个驱动器一侧没有加密/用户密钥保管，你所做的一切都会上传到 Microsoft，他们会维护密钥，他们只雇用通过背景调查的人员来运行基础设施，仅限美国国民等
I really have never heard of on prem 365 deployments, I think any confidentiality is handled via contracted promises with legal ramifications for breaking. With Azure GovCloud for instance there’s no encryption / user key custody on the one drive side, everything you do is uploaded to Microsoft and they maintain keys, they just hire people who passed a background check to run the infrastructure, US nationals only etc

Spooky23 2026/03/27 08:26
有预置办公室。

政府和365很奇怪。

非军事实体使用“政府社区云”，这是一种数据存储在微软数据中心分段区域的环境，但其他一切都在商业基础设施上。

你绝对 能 作为客户托管密钥。

微软处理所有这些事情的方法是疯狂的。

There is on prem office.

Government and 365 is weird.

Non-military entities use “Government Community Cloud”, which is an environment where data is stored in segmented areas of Microsoft data centers, but everything else is on commercial infrastructure.

You absolutely can host keys as a customer.

The Microsoft approach to all of this stuff is insane.

JumpCrisscross 2026/03/27 08:02
> Palantir 构建了客户用来处理自己数据的软件

在 Palantir 资助的 DOGE 运动之后 [1]，我认为人们有理由怀疑这些公司被极端分子渗透的程度。有人会说服自己，向 ICE 或骄傲男孩导出数据——就像每个病历显示自己是同性恋、接受过割礼或堕胎的纽约人的名字一样——是正确的做法。 （或者至少是有趣且无关紧要的。）

这是一个风险。不是结论。但鉴于 Palantir 的产品差异化日益缩小，我认为人们寻找替代品是公平的。

[1] https://www.wired.com/story/palantir-doge-irs-mega-api-data/

> Palantir builds software that customers use to work with their own data

After DOGE, a movement Palantir aided [1], I think it's fair for folks to wonder to what degree these firms have been infiltrated by extremists. Someone who will convince themselves that exporting data to ICE or the Proud Boys—like the names of every New Yorker whose medical records say they are gay, circumcised or have had an abortion—is the right thing to do. (Or at least funny and inconsequential.)

It's a risk. Not a conclusion. But given Palantir's offering is becoming less differentiated by the day, I think it's fair for people to look for alternatives.

[1] https://www.wired.com/story/palantir-doge-irs-mega-api-data/

easterncalculus 2026/03/27 06:17

在 HN 中查看
nradov 2026/03/27 08:26
公平地说，当 Microsoft 大约 40 年前首次定义 XLS 文件格式时，所有竞争对手也都使用专有文件格式。当时，复杂的结构化数据的开放文件格式还不是真正的问题。我想理论上他们可以使用 SGML，但考虑到当时硬件资源严重有限，这不太实用。
In fairness to Microsoft, when the XLS file format was first defined about 40 years ago all of their competitors also used proprietary file formats. Back then open file formats for complex, structured data weren't really a thing. I suppose in theory they could have used SGML but that wouldn't have been very practical given the severely limited hardware resources at the time.

text0404 2026/03/27 08:13
人们更关心的是 Palantir 围绕其服务领域创建的工具。他们使用未经证实的技术来分析、预测和制定决策。 Palantir 控制着洞察力、模型和结果，考虑到创始人的反民主和坦率的精神错乱的极端主义世界观，让他们为敏感和微妙的数据创建工具，这些数据会产生生死攸关的后果，这是非常令人担忧的。
The concern is more with the tools that Palantir creates around the domains they service. They analyze, predict, and shape decisions using unproven technology. Palantir controls insights, models, and outcomes, and given the anti-democratic and frankly unhinged extremist worldviews of the founders, it's highly concerning to allow them to create tools for sensitive and nuanced data that have life or death consequences.

在 HN 中查看
text0404 2026/03/27 13:26
Palantir 的产品不是 Excel。 Palantir 的创始人和高管非常清楚他们的工具的设计用途和功能，并且他们为自己的角色感到自豪。

据我所知，微软高管并没有吹嘘 Excel 被用来创建统一的“杀伤链”[1]，也没有推销旨在瞄准大规模杀伤性武器的软件 [2]，也没有声称他们的产品是为致命军事行动而设计的 [3]。

尽管你很想放弃他们的角色，但战争奸商就是战争奸商。 IBM 在二战期间也曾只制造计算机来管理供应链；他们把它卖给谁、它的用途以及他们为什么卖它仍然很重要。根据采访，Thiel 和 Karp 对他们在军工联合体中的作用感到高兴并接受它 [4]，因此将他们的产品比作 Excel 充其量是不诚实的。

[1] https://www.palantir.com/platforms/gotham/

[2] https://www.theguardian.com/news/2026/mar/26/ai-got-the-blam...

[3] https://www.heise.de/en/news/Palantir-defends-its-role-in-th...

[4] https://futurism.com/future-society/palantir-ceo-war-crimes

Palantir's products are not Excel. Palantir's founders and executives are well aware of what their tools are designed for and what they enable, and they're proud of their role.

As far as I know, Microsoft executives don't brag about Excel being used to create a unified "kill chain" [1], nor do they market software intended for targeting for weapons of mass destruction [2], nor do they claim that their products are designed for use in lethal military operations [3].

As much as you'd like to hand-waive away their role, a war profiteer is a war profiteer. IBM also used to just make computers to manage supply chains in WW2; who they sold it to, the purposes it was used for, and why they sold it is still important. Based on interviews, Thiel and Karp are gleeful about their role in the military-industrial complex and embrace it [4], so likening their products to Excel is disingenuous at best.

[1] https://www.palantir.com/platforms/gotham/

[2] https://www.theguardian.com/news/2026/mar/26/ai-got-the-blam...

[3] https://www.heise.de/en/news/Palantir-defends-its-role-in-th...

[4] https://futurism.com/future-society/palantir-ceo-war-crimes

presentation 2026/03/27 07:50
在不知道 Palantir 是什么的情况下憎恨 Palantir 是一种时髦的做法。他们的领导人有毒，这对案件没有帮助，但核心问题实际上是在这种政治气候下，西方世界各地的人们都不信任他们的政府，而且不信任任何赚钱的人以及科技公司（尤其是那些涉及数据和人工智能相关业务的科技公司）也是一种趋势，因此 Palantir 让这些不信任的参与者在赚钱的同时变得更有能力，这一事实被视为站在魔鬼一边。

所以这是一个信任问题，如果政府被认为是有效且有价值的，那么我希望他们有效，其中包括有效使用他们收集的数据。在这种氛围下，时髦人士宁愿他们的腐败政府也完全无能力限制腐败的影响。

Hating Palantir without having any idea of what they are is the trendy thing to do. Their leaders are toxic which doesn’t help the case, but the core issue really is just that in this political climate, people all over the western world don’t trust their governments, and it’s also trendy to distrust anyone making money, as well as tech companies - especially those involved in data and AI related businesses - so the fact that Palantir makes these distrusted actors more competent while making money doing it, is seen as siding with the devil.

So it’s a trust problem, if the government were seen as effective and worthy then I want them to be effective, which includes using the data they collect effectively. In this climate trendy people would prefer that their corrupt government is also fully incompetent to limit the effect of the corruption.

btown 2026/03/27 07:15
你的观点得到了很好的理解，但值得指出的是，昨天 Palantir 与他人共同获得了一份建造轨道武器系统的合同 [0]。

更广泛的观点是 Palantir 的具体融合：

- 访问跨行业孤岛的精细、非匿名数据

- 其主席的具体亲独裁使命（如此尖锐，以至于天主教会在几天前感到有必要进行具体谴责[1]）

- 如果采取尽可能广泛的解读，那么其货币风险可以说是最小化的监管环境。 HIPAA 提及“书面行政请求”的执法例外情况 [2]

-记录了对治理的担忧[3]

那些关心这种融合的人远非阴谋论者，并且可能对保护例如他们的医院网络的公众声誉以及服务能力——更不用说他们保护患者隐私的愿望了。

[0] https://www.usnews.com/news/top-news/articles/2026-03-24/and...

[1] https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/17/world/europe/peter-thiel-... - https://archive.is/2EOXa

[2] https://www.hhs.gov/hipaa/for-professionals/faq/505/what-doe...

[3] https://comptroller.nyc.gov/reports/letter-to-palantir-techn...

Your point is well taken, though it's worth pointing out that literally yesterday Palantir was co-awarded a contract for building orbital weapons systems [0].

The broader point is Palantir's specific confluence of:

- access to granular, non-anonymized data across industry silos

- its chairman's specific pro-authoritarian mission (so pointedly so that the Catholic Church felt the need to make a specific rebuke a few days ago [1])

- a regulatory environment in which its monetary risks are arguably minimized if it takes the broadest possible reading of e.g. HIPAA's law enforcement exceptions that mention "written administrative requests" [2]

- documented concerns about governance [3]

Those concerned with this confluence are far from conspiracy theorists, and may be quite rationally interested in protecting e.g. the public reputation of their hospital networks, and ability to service - to say nothing of their desire to protect the privacy of their patients.

[0] https://www.usnews.com/news/top-news/articles/2026-03-24/and...

[1] https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/17/world/europe/peter-thiel-... - https://archive.is/2EOXa

[2] https://www.hhs.gov/hipaa/for-professionals/faq/505/what-doe...

[3] https://comptroller.nyc.gov/reports/letter-to-palantir-techn...

在 HN 中查看
remarkEon 2026/03/27 08:39
有充分证据表明，从陆基系统发射的中程弹道导弹技术正在激增。用陆基发射器很难拦截它们。但是，如果从轨道上发射，命中的概率会更高。问题是，开发和实施这样的系统 a) 极其复杂，b) 成本非常昂贵。帕兰提尔和安杜里尔登场。

这个论点的分量取决于你对处于中程弹道导弹射程内的关心程度、你认为中程弹道导弹成为未来冲突决定性因素的可能性有多大，以及你是否希望美国在这场潜在冲突中取得胜利。许多人并不关心这种威胁，不认为中程弹道导弹很重要，和/或希望美国失败。我不是那些人中的一员。

There's ample evidence that medium range ballistic missile technology is proliferating, fired from land based systems. It is difficult to intercept these with ground-based launchers. But, if incepting from orbit the probability you score a hit is higher. The catch is that it is a) extremely complex, and b) very expensive to develop and implement a system like this. Enter Palantir and Anduril.

The weight of this argument rests on how much you care about being in range of MRBMs, how likely you think it is that MRBMs will be a decisive factor in a future conflict, and whether or not you want the United States to be victorious in this potential conflict. Many people do not care about this threat, don't think MRBMs will matter, and/or want the United States to lose. I am not one of those people.

twelve40 2026/03/27 08:13

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Outer_Space_Treaty

“禁止该条约缔约国在地球轨道上放置大规模杀伤性武器，将其安装在月球或任何其他天体上，或以其他方式将其部署在外层空间”

但是 1. 今天的观点是：让这些条约见鬼去吧，2. 你提到的还不是大规模杀伤性武器，所以我们都很好！

well, there _is_ this:

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Outer_Space_Treaty

"bars states party to the treaty from placing weapons of mass destruction in Earth orbit, installing them on the Moon or any other celestial body, or otherwise stationing them in outer space"

but 1. today's sentiment is: to hell with these treaties-schmeaties, and 2. what you mentioned is not yet a weapon of _mass_ destruction, so we're all good!

在 HN 中查看
beepbooptheory 2026/03/27 07:51
我认为您可能对最初的主张解读过多，而没有听到后续内容。 这里有两件事：1.公司的整体特征、广泛的章程和人员，以及2.它是非法或有害数据贩运的特定代理人的理论。 当然，我认为如果我们必须假设这些人和他们签订的合同是善意的，那么我们可以在这里完全拿走 2，但这仍然留下了 1，这是相当大的。 Like 1 回答你的后续问题 为什么 不管怎样，每个人都讨厌他们，但你仍然试图反问它与2有什么关系。这有道理吗？

真的，我认为没有人想在你怀疑的时候说“哦，甜蜜的夏天孩子”，但它是 真的非常难 现在不想只是……在世界各地做出手势，问为什么你仍然相信某种神圣性或绝对正确的东西，比如法律合同或其他各种形式的法律上的“责任”，当涉及到科技公司时，尤其是像这样大的公司。

I think you are maybe reading into the initial claim too much and not hearing the follow ups. There are two things here: 1. the overall character, broad charter, and people that compose the company, and 2. the theory that it is a specific agent in illegal or harmful data trafficking. And sure, I think we can take 2 away completely here if we simply must assume good faith from these guys and the contracts that they make, but that still kinda leaves 1 which is pretty big. Like 1 answers your follow up question of why everyone hates them either way, but you still are countering it by trying to ask what it has to do with 2. If that makes sense?

And really, I don't think anyone wants to "oh sweet summer child" you in your doubts here, but it's really extremely hard to not want to just... gesture around the world right now and ask why you still believe in some kind of sanctity or infallibility of something like the legal contract or other various forms of de jure "accountability" when it comes to tech companies, especially one as big as this.

Manuel_D 2026/03/27 08:01
人们声称 Palantir 可以访问私人信息，然后又退回到“我不喜欢该公司的性格”之类的说法，这种模式正是让我相信人们实际上对该公司并没有具体的抱怨。
This pattern in which people make claims about Palantir having access to private information, then retreat back to something along the lines of "I don't like the character of the company" is exactly the kind of thing that leads me to believe people don't actually have tangible complaints with the company.

remarkEon 2026/03/27 08:42
这是事实，但 Palantir 也以一种会引起怀疑和困惑的方式描述了他们的工作。当他们谈论本体作为客户环境的“数字孪生”时，人们可能会认为这实际上意味着 Palantir 正在窃取客户数据并克隆它，但事实并非如此。
This is true, but Palantir also describes what they do in a way that is going to cause skepticism and confusion. When they talk about the ontology acting as a "digital twin" of the customer environment one could be forgiven for thinking this does actually mean Palantir is exfiltrating customer data and cloning it, which is not what happens.

Manuel_D 2026/03/27 08:51
> 当他们谈论本体作为客户环境的“数字孪生”时，人们可能会认为这实际上意味着 Palantir 正在窃取客户数据并克隆它，但事实并非如此。

这基本上是说您的开发环境中的数据库架构与生产环境中的数据库架构相同。如果有人在逻辑上实现了这种飞跃，我会得出结论，他们几乎没有技术知识。

> When they talk about the ontology acting as a "digital twin" of the customer environment one could be forgiven for thinking this does actually mean Palantir is exfiltrating customer data and cloning it, which is not what happens.

This is basically saying you have the same DB schema on your dev environment as you do on prod. If anyone made that kind leap in logic, I would conclude they have little to no technical know how.

WatchDog 2026/03/27 08:47
您可以在本地运行它，实际上可以在技术上强制执行数据托管。

使用云托管产品的托管强制执行主要是合同性的，尽管它们确实提供了一些技术功能，例如使用客户 AWS 账户中的 AWS KMS 密钥加密所有数据。

尽管如此，这仍然依赖于相信他们不会制作自己单独的数据副本。

You can run it on-prem, where you can actually technologically enforce data custody.

Custody enforcement using the cloud hosted product, is mostly contractual, although they do offer some technical features, like encrypting all data using a AWS KMS key in the customer's AWS account.

Still, this relies on trusting that they won't make their own separate copies of the data.

guywithahat 2026/03/27 06:18
在某些方面，我几乎宁愿 Palantir 运行它，因为 DoW 会迫使他们实施非常严格的数据隔离功能，然后医院可以免费获得这些功能。我无法想象 Epic Healthcare Systems 会被迫如此积极地隔离数据。

nradov 2026/03/27 06:23
大多数 Epic 产品都会积极隔离数据。大多数实例都在本地运行，甚至托管在云平台上的实例也是单租户。他们在数据安全和隐私方面有良好的记录；据我所知，所有 Epic 数据泄露实际上都是由其他客户系统的渗透造成的。
Most Epic products aggressively isolate data. The majority of instances are run on-premises, and even those hosted on cloud platforms are single-tenant. They have a good record for data security and privacy; afaik all Epic data breaches were actually caused by infiltration of other customer systems.

paxys 2026/03/27 06:15
Palantir 是一家著名的 IT 咨询公司。你告诉他们“我想要一个管理病人记录的系统”，他们会派遣一个刚从大学毕业的工程师团队为你构建它，同时收取高价。由于游说和影响力，他们能够获得政府和军事合同，但通常你在网上看到的有关他们的一切都是营销。
Palantir is a glorified IT consulting company. You tell them "I want a system to manage patient records" and they will dispatch a team of engineers fresh out of college to build it for you while charging top dollar. They are able to get government & military contracts because of lobbying and influence, but generally everything you see about them online is marketing.

dmix 2026/03/27 05:21
> 然后解释他们如何进行监控和分析

他们与执法机构合作，帮助他们处理合法收集到其他政府数据库中的数据。他们的主要产品是合并来自各种数据库的数据并添加 UI 层进行分析。

基本上，Palantir 是一家数据集成公司，根据合同为政府和大型企业提供服务。他们受雇处理的一些数据包括监视数据和军事情报收集。

> Then explain how they do surveillance and analytics

They work with law enforcement agencies and help them process data they legally collect into other government databases. Their main product is merging data from various databases and adding a UI layer for analysis.

Basically, Palantir is a data integration company that works for government and larges businesses under contract. Some data they get hired to work on includes surveillance data and military intelligence collection.

在 HN 中查看
whiterose1214 2026/03/27 06:41
有一个奇怪的阴谋正在流传，Palantir 是世界上某种无所不知的力量，试图将每个人变成无意识的无人机。事实上，无论你喜欢与否，这只是一家在与政府和大公司签订大量合同方面相对成功的公司。

在医疗保健领域，内部定价和患者数据受到法律的严格保护。如果 Palantir 真的像你声称的那样监控你的医疗数据，那将是极其违法的，规模更大、影响力更大的公司将有强有力的法律依据起诉它，使其被遗忘。如果您认为 Palantir 对政府有着严格的控制，请考虑一下与其合作的卫生系统的影响——其中一些是其所在州最大的雇主。如果捐赠较少的小公司以某种方式对大公司施加不受限制的控制，那么一家全能公司可以控制政府的想法就没有意义。

当然，这些担忧大多源于两件事：（1）其自主战争的方式和（2）对移民监视的担忧。无论你喜欢与否，自主战争即将到来。 Palantir 在这方面的角色与其在商业领域的工作无关——除非你暗示他们通过不向企业客户销售高度先进的机器人技术来抑制潜在收入。对移民监控的担忧也有些言过其实，因为 Palantir 在法律上不能使用其商业工作的数据（除非其客户之一的合同管理严重不善）。在这种情况下，你真正应该批评的是美国政府，而不是仅仅想赚钱的承包商。

There's a bizarre conspiracy going around that Palantir is some all-seeing force in the world trying to turn everyone into mindless drones. In reality, it's just a company that—like it or not—has been relatively successful at securing massive contracts with the government and major corporations.

In a healthcare context, internal pricing and patient data are heavily protected by law. If Palantir were as guilty of surveilling your medical data as you allege, that would be tremendously illegal, and companies much larger and more influential would have strong legal grounds to sue it into oblivion. If you think Palantir has a tight grip on the government, consider the influence of the health systems it works with—some of which are the largest employers in their states. The idea that an all-powerful company can control the government doesn’t make sense if smaller companies, which donate less, are somehow exerting unchecked control over larger ones.

Of course, most of these concerns stem from two things: (1) its approach to autonomous warfare and (2) concerns about immigration surveillance. Autonomous warfare is coming, whether you like it or not. Palantir’s role in that is not related to its work in the commercial sector—unless you're suggesting they’re holding back potential revenue by not selling highly advanced robotics to corporate clients. Concerns about immigration surveillance are also somewhat overstated because, again, Palantir legally cannot use data from its commercial work (unless one of its clients severely mismanaged their contract). In that case, it’s really the U.S. government you should be criticizing — not the contractor simply trying to make money.

我不认为人们指责 Palantir 犯罪地 滥用数据。 政府围绕这些分析公司的能力重写了法律，因此 Palantir 在该领域开展业务。 无论是否“非法”，都不能改变他们所做的事情是令人毛骨悚然的“老大哥”的事实。

还有废话，他们不存储数据。

I don't think people are accusing Palantir of criminally misusing the data. The government rewrites the laws around what these analytics firms are capable of, and as such Palantir operates in that space. Whether or not it's "illegal" doesn't change the fact that what they're doing is creepy Big Brother shit.

Also bullshit that they don't store data.
